CT104597: Over 1,100 Watch Cap 2.0s have gone through our embroidery heads — it's the beanie Northwest crews actually ask for by name. The tight Carhartt knit takes a surprisingly sharp small logo, and the fold-up cuff doubles the decoration space. Companies buy them by the box every fall and they're gone by December.
CT103056: The Rugged Professional Series Cap is our best-selling Carhartt hat — nearly 400 embroidered across 50-plus orders. It's the structured everyday cap with the Carhartt patch pedigree; we embroider logos on the front panel or sew a leatherette patch for the full workwear look.
CT103938: The Cotton Canvas Cap is Carhartt's classic unstructured hat — the broken-in dad-cap silhouette with duck-canvas toughness. Two hundred have left our shop with logos; it suits a low-profile embroidered mark or a stitched-down patch equally well.
CTA205: The Acrylic Knit Hat — the A205 with the ear-flap-free classic watch profile — is Carhartt's traditional beanie, and one of the highest-margin headwear pieces we run. The cuff takes a tight embroidered logo; crews wear them from the first cold morning to the last.
CT106687: Carhartt's current Rugged Professional Series Cap — the structured work cap, refreshed. Two hundred-plus have already come through, most in whole-crew batches. Front-panel embroidery or a leatherette patch both land clean.
CT105298: The Canvas Mesh Back Cap is Carhartt's trucker — duck canvas up front, mesh behind, built for hot months over hard work. The flat front panel is prime real estate for an embroidered logo or a stitched patch; 130-plus decorated here so far.
CTA207: The Fleece Hat is the softest of Carhartt's beanies — warm, quick to pull on, easy under a hood or hard hat liner. It embroiders cleanly on the cuff and makes an easy add-on to any winter crew order.
CT106665: The current-generation Ashland Cap — Carhartt's relaxed canvas everyday hat, updated. It's the casual counterpart to the structured Rugged Pro; a small front embroidered mark keeps it wearable off the clock, which is where caps earn their advertising.
CT101604: The Ashland Cap is Carhartt's soft, unstructured canvas hat — the one that looks broken-in from the first wear. A classic canvas for a low-key embroidered logo; crews grab them for the truck, the yard, and the weekend.
CT106577: The Carhartt Flexfit 110 Mesh Back Cap pairs the Flexfit band with a Carhartt duck front — fitted comfort in two sizes that cover a whole crew, mesh for the warm months. Seventeen separate orders and counting; the structured front takes a dimensional embroidered logo beautifully.
CT104485: The Knit Insulated Face Mask is Carhartt's cold-weather face coverage — a lined knit mask for the crews that work through the coldest mornings of the year. An easy add-on line in a winter gear order.
CTA202: The Fleece 2-In-1 Headwear converts from beanie to neck gaiter — one piece covering two winter needs. Crews running lean gear lists like it; a small embroidered mark on the fleece keeps it in the program.
CTA267: The Force Helmet-Liner Mask is built to run under a hard hat — fast-drying Force fabric covering head and face without bulk. Winter site work under a shell; a practical add-on to cold-weather crew orders.
CT102368: The Firm Duck Hood is old-school Carhartt problem-solving — a duck-canvas hood that adds weather coverage to hoodless jackets and vests. Niche, tough, and exactly what certain crews have been asking for since before we stocked it.
CT105086: The Cotton Blend Filter Pocket Gaiter is Carhartt's work-grade neck gaiter with a filter pocket — dust and cold coverage for grinding, demolition, and winter yards. Sold in crew quantities as a gear-order add-on.
CT105160: Carhartt's Cotton Ear Loop Face Masks in a three-pack — simple washable work-site face coverage with the duck-tough reputation behind it. A utility line item for shop and site orders.
CTB0000486: The 28L Foundry Series Dual-Compartment Backpack is the big brother of Carhartt's work-pack line — laptop-safe up top, gear below, built like their jackets. We embroidered a 200-plus batch for one company gift program and the feedback wrote our recommendation: nobody leaves a Carhartt backpack in a closet.
CT89176508: The Foundry Series Pro Backpack is the most-ordered bag in our Carhartt program — nearly 40 separate orders, most as employee gifts and new-hire kits. Rugged construction, laptop sleeve, and a front panel that takes an embroidered logo like it was measured for it.
CT89132109: The Backpack 20-Can Cooler is the piece that sells itself at the job site — a Carhartt-tough cooler you wear on your back up the scaffolding. A hundred-plus have gone out with logos; it's a favorite summer crew gift with a genuinely long service life.
CT89251601: The Lunch 6-Can Cooler is the everyday carry of Carhartt's cooler line — insulated, duck-tough, sized for one person's lunch and then some. Companies order them as onboarding gifts and safety-milestone rewards; embroidery on the front flap makes it official.
CT89350303: The Foundry Series Backpack is the standard-issue model of Carhartt's pack line — everyday capacity, work-grade materials, and 20-plus separate orders through our shop. It embroiders cleanly and survives years of truck-floor duty.
CTB0000484: The 18-Pocket Utility Roll is a tradesperson's tool organizer in Carhartt duck — rolls flat, carries a full hand-tool spread, and outlasts the pouches it replaces. We ran a 75-piece logoed batch for one crew; it's a distinctive gift that actually gets used.
CT89032822: The Vertical 12-Can Cooler splits the difference between lunchbox and party cooler — tall, tough, and easy to carry to the site or the boat. Twenty-plus orders, mostly gifts; the front panel takes an embroidered company mark.
CT89241804: The Canvas Backpack is Carhartt's classic-look everyday pack — duck canvas outside, modern organization inside. Seventeen separate orders tells the story: companies buy a few at a time as standout employee gifts.
CT89520701: The Duffel 36-Can Cooler is the crew-size cooler of the line — duffel-style carry, enough capacity for the whole truck on a Friday. It's a company-picnic and crew-gift standby with room for a bold embroidered logo.
CT89098101: The Carhartt Waist Pack is the tool-belt generation's everyday carry — duck-tough, right-sized for phone, tape, and knife. Fifteen separate orders, usually as stocking-stuffer-grade crew gifts with an embroidered mark.
CT89260209: The Foundry Series 20-inch Duffel is the gym-and-gear bag of Carhartt's line — work-grade materials in a weekender size. Companies gift them logoed for milestones; they hold up to years of job-site commutes.
CT89121325: The Utility Tote is Carhartt duck in open-top carry-everything form — the shop-runner's bag for parts, cords, and lunch. An embroidered logo on the side panel turns it into rolling advertising at the supply house.
CT89105112: The Canvas Packable Duffel with Pouch stows into its own pouch until needed — a Carhartt-tough overflow bag for travel and gear hauls. A clever logoed gift that surprises people with how much it swallows.
CT89240105: The Foundry Series 14-inch Tool Bag brings Carhartt construction to the classic electrician's bag — wide mouth, hard bottom, duck-tough shell. Logoed, it's the crew gift that gets used daily for a decade.
CTB0000607: The Two-Compartment Cooler Backpack separates food from gear in one wearable pack — Carhartt's evolution of the cooler-you-can-climb-with. An easy pick for summer crew gifts with an embroidered front panel.
CTB0000564: The current Canvas Packable Duffel with Pouch — Carhartt's stuff-it-away travel duffel, refreshed. Packs to nothing, carries everything, takes a logo on the side panel.
CTB0000481: The 25L Ripstop Backpack is the lightest pack in Carhartt's line — ripstop fabric instead of duck, everyday capacity, work-brand bones. A modern-looking logoed gift for teams that skew younger.
CTB0000482: The Crossbody Zip Bag is Carhartt's compact sling — phone, wallet, keys, done. It's the small logoed gift with big daily-wear time; the front panel takes a tight embroidered mark.
CTB0000487: The 120L Foundry Series Duffel is the biggest bag Carhartt makes — deployment-scale capacity in work-grade materials. Crews traveling to remote jobs live out of these; a logoed batch makes the baggage carousel easy.
CTB0000605: The 75L Heavy Haul Duffel is the workhorse traveler of the line — huge capacity, haul-anything handles, Carhartt toughness. For travel crews and serious gear hauls, with room for a bold logo.
CTB0000606: The Crossbody Snap Bag is the smallest carry in Carhartt's bag line — a snap-closure sling for the essentials. A distinctive small gift with the label people recognize.
CT89101701: The Tote 18-Can Cooler is the open-carry cooler of the line — tote convenience, real insulation, Carhartt duck shell. Company picnics and summer crew gifts are its natural habitat.
CTC0001607: The Nylon Duck Organizer is Carhartt's answer to the junk-drawer truck cab — pockets and structure in a tough little organizer. A niche but beloved add-on in fleet and crew-gift orders.
CTC0001617: The Universal Seat Organizer hangs Carhartt-grade storage on a truck seat back — clipboard, tablet, tools, paperwork. Fleet managers order them logoed alongside apparel programs.
CTGD0794: The High-Dexterity Open-Cuff Glove is Carhartt's work glove for jobs that need fingertip feel — snug fit, open cuff, real durability. We sell them as gear-order add-ons; nearly a hundred pairs went out in recent crew kits.
CTGF0622: The Thermal-Lined Fleece Touch-Sensitive Glove works a phone screen without coming off — warm fleece for winter yards and delivery routes. An easy add-on line in cold-weather gear orders.
CTGL0511: The Waterproof Insulated Glove is Carhartt's full-winter hand protection — waterproof shell, insulated core, built for wet Northwest cold. Crews add pairs to winter orders alongside the jackets.
CTGC0703: The C-Grip Glove is the everyday grip glove of Carhartt's line — coated palms for material handling at a crew-quantity price. A practical add-on to any site-gear order.
CTSC1054: Carhartt's Heavyweight Crew Socks in a four-pack — boot-grade socks with the same over-built reputation as the jackets. They ride along in winter gear orders and new-hire kits.
CTSC4223: The Force Midweight Crew Socks in a three-pack — moisture-managing work socks from Carhartt's performance line. A small line item that rounds out a full crew kit.
CTP0000502: The Firm Duck Sherpa-Lined Blanket is Carhartt's living-room crossover — duck canvas one side, sherpa the other, built like their coats. Embroidered in a corner, it's one of the most-kept company gifts we decorate.
CTP0000505: The Dog Chore Coat puts Carhartt duck on the crew dog — a real insulated work coat cut for four legs. It's the guaranteed hit of any company gift program; add an embroidered company mark and the shop dog is officially on payroll.
